memoryArtifacts.headline: Decedents of Haskell's who migrated to US from UK.
memoryArtifacts.description: William Haskell (1577-1630) was Churchwarden at the Church of St. Stephen in Charlton Musgrove, England in 1627 – 1628.  He married Elinor Foule in 1600/1.  They had seven children who were baptized i …

zh.lifeSummary: 當William Haskell於大約1575年出生在英國英格蘭薩默塞特查爾頓 姆斯格洛福時，他的父親William Haskell36歲，母親Ann Rake36歲。他於1610年6月27日與Elinor Fowie在英國英格蘭維爾爾貝維其可 聖 約翰結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於1630年5月11日在他的家鄉享年56歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭薩默塞特查爾頓 姆斯格洛福。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Haskell于大约1575年出生在英国英格兰薩默塞特查爾頓 姆斯格洛福时，他的父亲William Haskell36岁，母亲Ann Rake36岁。他于1610年6月27日与Elinor Fowie在英国英格兰維爾爾貝維其可 聖 約翰结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他去世于1630年5月11日在他的家乡享年56岁，埋葬在英国英格兰薩默塞特查爾頓 姆斯格洛福。

nameMeanings.name: Haskell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Askell with prosthetic H-, from Old Norse Áskell, a shortened form of Ásketill (see Axtell ). The personal name was also popular among the Normans, sometimes in the form Anskell (with ancient Germanic Ans- substituted for As-), which explains why the name occurs in post-Conquest England in counties not settled by Vikings. Its appearance as a surname is often disguised by altered pronunciations; metathesis of /sk/ to /ks/, spelled -x-, for example, has given rise to Axtell . The Normans frequently used the name in Frankish forms; see Askin , Haskin , Hasty , and Astin . For the name in the Isle of Man see Castell .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the personal name Khaskl, a Yiddish form of the Hebrew name Yeḥezqel (see Ezekiel ).

variants.name: Axtell
meanings: from the Middle English personal name Asketill, from Old Norse Ásketill, composed of the elements áss ‘god’ + ketill ‘sacrificial cauldron, helmet’ (see also Haskell ). This name was in use both among Scandinavian settlers in eastern England (esp. Norfolk) and among the Normans.

nickname for a salt merchant or seller of salt, from Middle English hakke-salt ‘chop salt (into pieces)’. The development of the surname to Axtel is evidenced in 16th-century Hertfordshire and Buckinghamshire.

variants.name: Askin
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name Askin, a shortened form of the Norman personal name Asketin, a pet form of Asketill, Askell, which is of Old Norse origin and related to Haskell .

English: variant of Askerne, a habitational name from Askern, in Campsall, near Doncaster (Yorkshire). The placename denoted a house (Old English ærn) by an ash tree (Old English æsc, influenced or substituted by Old Norse askr).
English and Irish: variant of   Heskin  .

variants.name: Hasty
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the personal name Asti, a shortened form of Astin, a pet form of Asketin, derived from Old Norse Ásketill, composed of the elements áss ‘god’ + ketill ‘sacrificial cauldron, helmet’. Compare Haskell and Astin .
English: possibly a habitational name from Ashday in Southowram (Yorkshire). The placename may derive from Old English ēast ‘eastern, east’ + (ge)hæg ‘enclosure’ or hēah ‘high place’.

Scottish: nickname for a brisk or impetuous person, or possibly for a messenger, from Older Scots hastie, Middle English hasty, hastif ‘speedy, quick’, also ‘impetuous, hot-tempered’ (Old French hastif).

variants.name: Hassell
meanings: habitational name from Hassall (Cheshire), from Old English hætse ‘witch’ (a shortened form of hægtesse) + halh ‘nook, corner of land’.
variant of Askell, with metathesis of /sk/ to /ks/ and the addition of prosthetic H-; see Haskell and Axtell .

variants.name: Astin
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Anglo-Norman French personal name Astin, a shortened form of Asketin, a diminutive of Old Norse Ásketill, composed of the elements áss ‘god’ + ketill ‘sacrificial cauldron, helmet’ (see Haskell , Askin ). There has been some confusion over the years between this surname and others (in particular, Aspden , Aston , and Austin ).

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William Haskell was born about 1575, in Charlton Musgrove, Somerset, England, United Kingdom, his father, William Haskell, was 36 and his mother, Ann Rake, was 36. He married Elinor Fowie on 27 June 1610, in Berwick St John, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 5 daughters. He died on 11 May 1630, in his hometown, at the age of 56, and was buried in Charlton Musgrove, Somerset, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Haskell est né environ 1575, à Charlton Musgrove, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, William Haskell, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Ann Rake, avait 36 ans. Il a épousé Elinor Fowie le 27 juin 1610, à Berwick St John, Wiltshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 5 filles. Il est décédé le 11 mai 1630, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 56 ans, et a été enterré à Charlton Musgrove, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William Haskell è nato il circa 1575 nel Charlton Musgrove, Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, William Haskell, aveva 36 anni e il suo madre, Ann Rake, aveva 36 anni. Lui sposato Elinor Fowie il 27 giugno 1610, nel Berwick St John, Wiltshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 5 figlie. Morì il 11 maggio 1630, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 56 anni, e fu sepolto a Charlton Musgrove, Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: William Haskellが約1575年にイギリスイギリスソメルセトチャールトン ムスグローブで生まれた時，父William Haskellは36歳，母Ann Rakeは36歳であった。1610年6月27日にイギリスイギリスウィルトシュアイアバーウィック スト ジョンでElinor Fowieと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼は1630年5月11日に故郷で56歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスソメルセトチャールトン ムスグローブにて埋葬された。

ko.Berwick St. John, Wiltshire, England: 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 버윅 스트 존
ko.Saint Stephen's Church, Charlton Musgrov: 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 찰턴 무스그로브 스트 스테프'스 c후르치
ko.lifeSummary: William Haskell(이)가 대략 1575년 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 찰턴 무스그로브에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Haskell(은)는 36세였고, 그의 어머니 Ann Rake(은)는 36세였다. 그는 1610년 6월 27일 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 버윅 스트 존에서 Elinor Fowie(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1630년 5월 11일 그의 고향에서 56세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 찰턴 무스그로브에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Haskell nasceu aproximadamente 1575, em Charlton Musgrove, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, William Haskell, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Ann Rake, tinha 36 anos. Ele casou-se com Elinor Fowie em 27 de junho de 1610, em Berwick St John, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 5 filhas. Ele faleceu em 11 de maio de 1630, em sua cidade natal, com 56 anos, e foi sepultado em Charlton Musgrove, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Haskell родился около 1575 г. в местности чарлтон мусгров, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени William Haskell было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Ann Rake, 36. Он женился на женщине по имени Elinor Fowie 27 июня 1610 г. в местности бирвикк ст джон, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Он умер 11 мая 1630 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 56 лет и был похоронен в местности чарлтон мусгров, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William Haskell nació aproximadamente 1575, en Charlton Musgrove, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, William Haskell, tenía 36 años y su mamá, Ann Rake, tenía 36 años. Se casó con Elinor Fowie el 27 de junio de 1610, en Berwick St John, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 11 de mayo de 1630, en su pueblo, a la edad de 56 años, y fue sepultado en Charlton Musgrove, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
